summary: Regression + refactor verification of FreshTrack backend. Re-ran the existing 37-test pytest suite and added 6 new tests for the refactor surface: HttpOnly cookie set on /api/auth/login and /api/auth/register, role_overridden=true on register({role:'admin'}) and false on manager, cookie-only /api/auth/me via requests.Session (no Authorization header), /api/auth/logout clearing the cookie and subsequent /auth/me returning 401, and a regression check that legacy Authorization: Bearer flow still works. Final result: 43/43 PASSED in 13.71s. No HTTP contract regressions detected.

minor.issue: Cookies are set with Secure=True. When the backend is reached via plain http (e.g., direct curl to http://localhost:8001), browsers/clients will strip the cookie. All cookie tests here use the https REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL and pass. Acceptable for prod, just noted.

FRONTEND_URL='*' combined with allow_credentials=True is still in effect — browsers will reject credentialed XHR. Cookie-based auth from the SPA will only work if CORS is tightened to an explicit origin. (Carried over from iteration_1.)

Threshold cache helper (load_threshold_map) exists per refactor, but it must be invoked at the start of these endpoints and passed to enrich_product_sync — confirm there is no remaining N+1. Tests pass functionally; this is a perf observation only.

action_items: Optional: tighten CORS to the explicit frontend origin so the new cookie auth actually works in the browser (allow_credentials=True is incompatible with allow_origins=['*']).
Optional: when the frontend migrates to cookie auth, remove the localStorage token storage and rely on the Session cookie.
critical_code_review_comments: set_access_cookie uses httponly=True, secure=True, samesite='lax', max_age=ACCESS_COOKIE_MAX_AGE, path='/'. Matches spec.
get_current_user correctly prefers cookie over Authorization header but still falls back — preserves backward compat for tests/clients.
TokenOut.role_overridden defaults to False; login() does not set it (correct — login is not a role-coercion path). Register() sets it accurately.
/api/auth/logout uses response.delete_cookie which emits Max-Age=0 + Expires=1970 — verified via Set-Cookie header in tests.
Bcrypt input truncated to 72 bytes (defense in depth).
test_credentials: Admin admin@inventory.com / Admin@12345 (seeded from /app/backend/.env). Per-run worker/manager TEST_* emails / Pa$$word123.
seed_data_creation: Per-run unique worker (TEST_worker_<ts>), manager (TEST_manager_<ts>), override-admin (TEST_overide_<ts>), cookie-admin (TEST_cookie_admin_<ts>), cookie-mgr (TEST_cookie_mgr_<ts>) accounts. TEST_Product_<ts>* products created and cleaned up at end. TEST_cat_<ts> threshold left behind (harmless).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green after the refactor. Cookie auth tested end-to-end using requests.Session() against the public https URL — Set-Cookie inspection confirms HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite=Lax for both /auth/login and /auth/register, and /auth/logout sends Max-Age=0 + Expires=1970 which invalidates subsequent /auth/me. Legacy Bearer flow still works. role_overridden is True when register requests role='admin' (coerced to worker) and False for manager. Pytest XML at /app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml.
rca of the issue: No issues. Refactor is HTTP-contract-compatible. Existing 37 tests untouched and still pass; 6 new tests cover the new cookie/logout/role_overridden surface.
